title: Living in Boulder vlog 4 - 22 - 2017: BouldeRoman Timeline

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a brief vlog update about working on his 'BouldeRoman' (Boulder Rome) wiki/website project, specifically converting and building a timeline that parallels the Roman historical timeline. He explains the page structure he's creating and mentions listening to Livy via LibriVox while working.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: This is his most important current project and he wants to build momentum on it this weekend
Finds it 'trippy' and interesting that his fictional Boulder-Rome calendar aligns with actual Roman dates
Intrigued by the legend and calls it interesting if it was a real prediction

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Right now, it's April 22nd, 2:52 PM.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on my Boulder Room page.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I divided the Boulder Room page into older and timeline sections, which took me two days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday, I was working on the timeline and converting dates.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: The Boulder timeline is the same as the wrong timeline, so you can use the same calendar.

claims.subject: Kyle's timeline
claims.claim: Every event in the Boulder timeline corresponds to the same year in other timelines.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In the year 762, there's a bottle mentioned in the Boulder timeline, which is trippy.

claims.subject: Boulder Room
claims.claim: The Boulder Room has a legend that predicts the last 1,000 years.

claims.subject: Boulder Room
claims.claim: The Boulder Room was the capital of Italy for 1,000 to 4,000 years.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm motivated to work on the Google Docs for the Boulder Room because I want to see how it looks.

claims.subject: Kyle's project
claims.claim: My Boulder Room project includes pages for timeline, time chart, language, tribes, cities, buildings, and battles.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm looking forward to working on the cities and tribes sections of the Boulder Room project.

claims.subject: Wikipedia
claims.claim: Wikipedia has a feature where you can type in a name and year to see events, which I plan to use for my timeline.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to focus on my Boulder Room project this weekend to build momentum.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to get my Boulder Room project to a point where I can estimate how long it will take.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The Boulder Room project is my most important project right now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have other tasks to do, like Google Docs and the wrong timeline.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm listening to Livy on LibriVox, which has a different writing style than my website.

claims.subject: LibriVox
claims.claim: The LibriVox readings are from a different book with the same language but different writing than my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have to find the context or mentions of people in the LibriVox readings on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't decided what to put in the background of my video.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder vlog for April 22nd. Right now it's April 22nd, 2.52pm. So right now I'm working on my Boulder Room page. I went in there and then I divided it up into Boulder Room and Time Chart, which I hadn't created, but it took me two days.
And then, well, yesterday I was doing a timeline, Boulder Room and Timeline, and I'll be done, I don't know, I'm converting, yeah, like the dates.
The interesting thing about my timeline is the Boulder Room and Timeline is the same as the Rome Timeline, so it uses the same calendar.
So every single thing that happens in my timeline at Boulder Room, it's the same year that everything else happens.
And so I'm actually saying the same year. When I read the website, I go, in the year 762, there's the battle of so-and-so.
Well, they were saying, yes, it's the year 762, and it's the battle, because I think that's pretty trippy.
And Rome, I think when they first started, there's a legend that when they first started, they had a prediction that Rome would last 1,000 years or something.
And Rome was the capital of Italy for 1,040 years, so that's interesting.
If this is a real prediction, Romans love their portents.
So, yeah, but after this, I'm going to be extremely motivated to do the Googler stuff, too, of Boulder Room,
Because I think I already got it started, and I really want to see what it looks like.
So I'll go into the other pages, you know, I have Boulder Room, Time Chart, Timeline, you know, the Boulder Room page, and Time Chart, Timeline.
And then Languages, Tribes, and Cities is a page, and then Buildings Within the Actual City is its own page.
And then Battles is its own page.
So, yeah, I'm really looking forward to doing the, you know, the cities around and the tribes around, so, after I convert the timeline.
But then I'll beef up the timeline.
There's this feature that's on Wikipedia that didn't exist when I was working on my timeline,
Where you just type in the name, the number, or the year, and it says stuff that happened, so I can plug in, you know.
That's what I'll do with my timeline.
Plug everything in that happened that I missed, and then, yeah, then connect it to Libby.
So this is a project that'll take a while, but this weekend, that's what I'm going to focus on.
It's because I'm just trying to get it up to speed.
I want to get it up to, get it, get the momentum going on it, and maybe even get to a point where I can wrap my head around it to see how long it'll take this weekend.
But, yeah, this is my new project.
This is the, actually, this is the big, big, most important one.
Um, but, uh, there are some other things I have to do, um, Google everything Rome and stuff and Boulder, but I don't think Boulder has that much time left.
So anyways, um, oh, and I'm listening to Libby, um, on the, on, uh, LibriVox.
The LibriVox Libby that the people read, they're reading it from a different book.
It's like totally different writing, same language, different writing than the one that's on my website.
So you have to, you can't, you can't, I can't find on here where they were just by quoting.
I have to find, you know, what they're talking about on my website if I want to find it, um, like on the context or if they mention a person because, yeah, it's all different.
It's interesting.
Okay, so let's see, how long, how long have I talked?
Have I talked long enough for the background?
Um, oh, I haven't decided.
Maybe I'll put something interesting.
Okay, peace out.
